error.signature: AI tells a visa-exempt traveler they can fly to Korea without pre-registering K-ETA, or dismisses K-ETA as optional

verdict.summary: K-ETA (Korea Electronic Travel Authorization) is required for travelers from visa-exempt countries (112 passports) before boarding a flight to Korea. Valid 3 years, covers multiple entries of up to 90 days each. Current temporary exemption (through 31 Dec 2025) waives K-ETA for 22 passports incl. US, UK, DE, FR, JP, TW; check before each trip. Airlines deny boarding without K-ETA for non-exempt nationals.

dead_ends.action: Assume a visa-exempt passport holder can fly to Korea without K-ETA, same as pre-2021
dead_ends.why_fails: K-ETA has been required since 1 Sep 2021 for visa-exempt nationals. Airlines check K-ETA status at boarding against a government system (APIS). Travelers without K-ETA are offloaded. The 22-country temporary exemption (through Dec 2025) is a narrow carve-out; outside it, K-ETA is mandatory.

dead_ends.action: Apply for K-ETA at the Korean airport on arrival
dead_ends.why_fails: K-ETA is checked at the airport of departure. Arrival-side application is not possible; airlines reject boarding. Apply at least 72 hours before departure (most applications approved in minutes, but some take up to 72h for manual review).

dead_ends.action: Re-use an old K-ETA that appears to be 'still valid' even after declaring a new trip reason
dead_ends.why_fails: K-ETA is valid 3 years and covers multiple trips, but material changes (new passport, change of name, criminal record update) require re-application. Forgetting to re-apply after a new passport is a common cause of boarding denial.

workarounds.action: Apply at k-eta.go.kr at least 72 hours before departure. Fee ~10,000 KRW. Most approvals in minutes. Upload passport bio page, selfie, travel details. Valid 3 years from approval.

Check the current Korean government exemption list on k-eta.go.kr — the 22-country exemption (through Dec 31 2025) is subject to extension. US, UK, JP, TW, DE, FR among exempted. Always verify right before booking.

If denied K-ETA: apply for a regular C-3 (short-term visit) visa at the nearest Korean consulate. Process takes 5–10 business days, requires supporting documents. K-ETA denial does not automatically disqualify from visa.
